description: Behavioral: a witness MUST NOT cosign a checkpoint that fails consistency against its retained head (RFC-ACDP-0015 §7 step 2 — the entire point of witnessing). The witness has previously observed and retained the genuine log-003 size-3 head (root_hash sha256:cf4604...). The registry now presents a signature-valid size-5 checkpoint whose root_hash has been REWRITTEN to sha256:dead...  (not the genuine log-001 size-5 root sha256:0b5978...), so the RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.2 consistency proof between the retained size-3 head and this size-5 checkpoint MUST fail. Expected: the witness refuses to cosign, persists both checkpoints and the failing proof as evidence, and (SHOULD) surfaces it. Behavioral — not executed by scripts/conformance-runner.py (no witness_test_keypair). The registry checkpoint signature itself verifying is not sufficient; consistency is the gate.

retained_head.note: The genuine log-003 size-3 checkpoint the witness observed earlier and stored (RFC-ACDP-0015 §7 retained head).

presented_checkpoint.note: A size-5 checkpoint whose root_hash was REWRITTEN. Its own registry signature is assumed valid (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3 passes); what fails is consistency (§9.2). The genuine log-001 size-5 root is sha256:0b5978172c671ca050b44790a749b18fc29d58a7a17495fbb4e0f86eb885f731 — this fabricated root differs, so no valid consistency proof from size 3 exists.

notes: RFC-ACDP-0015 §7 step 2: verify an RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.2 consistency proof between the retained size-3 head and the presented size-5 checkpoint BEFORE cosigning. No valid PROOF(3, D[5]) reproduces both the retained sha256:cf4604... root and the fabricated sha256:deadbeef... root, so consistency fails.
The witness MUST NOT emit a cosignature (RFC-ACDP-0015 §7): a witness that cosigns a root-rewrite provides negative security value.

The witness MUST persist both signature-valid-but-mutually-inconsistent checkpoints and the failing proof as evidence (RFC-ACDP-0015 §7, RFC-ACDP-0012 §15) and SHOULD surface a distinct non-cosigning status on GET /log/witness.

A consumer therefore never sees a cosignature for the rewritten checkpoint from an honest witness; the checkpoint stays 0-witnessed and the consumer's quorum policy (RFC-ACDP-0015 §8.1) declines to rely on it.
